name: Water Surface
summary: A Three.js-inspired Cesium polygon water shader with procedural normal distortion, Fresnel reflection, and refractive color blending.
useCases: River and lake surfaces with flowing wave motion
Water conservancy demos that need a reusable animated water polygon

polygon.description: WGS84 polygon positions with longitude and latitude.

color.description: CSS color used by the water shader.

distortionScale.description: Normal-distortion scale inspired by Three.js Water distortionScale.

refractionStrength.description: Strength of refractive underwater color blending.

fresnelPower.description: Power curve used by the Fresnel reflection term.

minimal.code:
import { createWaterSurfaceEffect } from '@ztgkzhaohao/geo-effect-kit'

const water = createWaterSurfaceEffect(viewer, {
  polygon: [
    { longitude: 116.332, latitude: 39.886 },
    { longitude: 116.454, latitude: 39.884 },
    { longitude: 116.491, latitude: 39.925 },
    { longitude: 116.346, latitude: 39.966 },
  ],
  type: 'river',
  color: '#3de7ff',
  waveStrength: 0.48,
  reflectionStrength: 0.36,
  distortionScale: 18,
  reflectivity: 0.58,
  refractionStrength: 0.42,
  fresnelPower: 4,
  flowDirection: 90,
})

water.flyTo()
water.destroy()

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
Coordinates are WGS84 longitude/latitude and are converted with Cartesian3.fromDegrees.
The water polygon uses GeoWaterSurfaceMaterial with czm_frameNumber animation, procedural normal distortion, Fresnel reflection, and refractive color blending.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the data source and render loop.
